title: Adjective
definition: An adjective is a word that describes or gives more information about a noun or pronoun.
formula: Adjective + Noun  OR  Subject + Linking Verb + Adjective
rules: Adjectives can come before a noun: a beautiful garden.
Adjectives can come after linking verbs like 'be', 'seem', 'look': She is happy.
Adjectives of quantity: many, few, some, several.
Adjectives of quality: beautiful, tall, honest, kind.
Comparative adjectives compare two things: taller, more beautiful.
Superlative adjectives compare three or more: tallest, most beautiful.
examples: He is the tallest boy in the class.
This coffee is stronger than that one.

commonMistakes.wrong: She is more taller than me.
commonMistakes.why: Don't use 'more' with a comparative form that already ends in '-er'.

commonMistakes.wrong: This is the most best movie.
commonMistakes.why: 'Best' is already superlative; don't add 'most' before it.

commonMistakes.wrong: He is a good in cricket.
commonMistakes.why: Use 'good at' (preposition), not 'a good in', to describe a skill.

quiz.question: Identify the adjective: 'This is a beautiful painting.'
Choose the correct comparative: 'This road is ___ than that one.'
She is the ___ (tall) student in class. (superlative)

quiz.sentence: He is more smarter than his brother.
quiz.correction: He is smarter than his brother.
quiz.explanation: Do not use 'more' with an '-er' comparative form.

quiz.question: Which sentence uses an adjective of quantity?
